item.title: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
item.description: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

item.title: Kanye offers to meet Jewish community in UK after Wireless controversy
item.description: He said his goal was to 'come to London and present a show of change' through his music.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump’s Deadline Draws Closer With No Sign of Diplomatic Breakthrough
item.description: Israel and Iran traded fresh attacks on Tuesday after President Trump said a cease-fire proposal was “not good enough.” Mr. Trump has threatened to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz by 8 p.m. Eastern.

item.title: Gunman Is Killed in Shootout Near Israeli Consulate in Istanbul, Official Says
item.description: Two other armed men were wounded in a clash with police officers, according to the regional governor.

item.title: More than 1,700 Brits who fell ill in Cape Verde join action against Tui
item.description: Tui is investigating the claims and says it is "not in a position to provide a statement at this stage".

item.title: Plan 2 student loan interest rates capped at 6% in England
item.description: The cap on Plan 2 and postgraduate loan interest rates comes amid a risk of rising inflation.

item.title: Student loan interest rates on controversial plans to be capped at 6%
item.description: Limit will apply to all plan 2 loans as well as plan 3 postgraduate loans

item.title: Air India CEO steps down early as losses mount
item.description: Wilson, whose term was set to end in 2027, will remain CEO and MD until a successor is appointed.

item.title: Italian government poised to replace Leonardo CEO who oversaw surge in orders
item.description: Move by Italian government comes as Roberto Cingolani’s three-year term at defence group expires next month

item.title: Citi sets aggressive targets for bankers in wealth management unit
item.description: Revised goals involve net revenue and value of assets clients entrust to Citi as business has lagged Wall Street peers

item.title: Ramdane Touhami is making the world’s most beautiful stationery
item.description: Because ‘sending letters is next-level luxury’

item.title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing offers to buy Universal Music in €55bn deal
item.description: Proposed transaction would combine world’s largest music group with a blank-cheque company

item.title: Pakistan Says It Hit a Military Target. Investigations Suggest It Was a Rehab Center.
item.description: After the deadliest attack in Afghanistan since the Taliban took over, families searched among photos and remains for signs of their relatives.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
Australia Charges Ex-Soldier With War Crimes in Afghanistan
item.description: The suspect, charged with five counts of murder, is accused of killing or ordering the killings of unarmed civilians during the Afghan war.

item.title: Ukraine Ramps Up Attacks on Russian Oil, Aiming to Curb Iran War Windfall
item.description: As the Persian Gulf conflict boosts the oil revenue that finances Moscow’s war against Ukraine, Kyiv’s forces are striking at Russia’s ability to refine and ship its crude.

item.title: Doctors’ demands could cost NHS up to £30bn a year, says Streeting
item.description: Health secretary warns that acceding to further pay rise would lead to wage claims from other staff, as latest six-day strike begins

item.title: Air India chief quits early after turbulent tenure
item.description: Campbell Wilson’s ambition to turn carrier into world-class airline marred by fatal crash of Boeing Dreamliner jet

item.title: Weakened and Reluctant, Yemen’s Houthis Belatedly Enter War
item.description: Analysts say their delay in supporting Iran is partly because their capabilities were severely degraded by the U.S.-Israeli campaign last year.

item.title: Better public transport alone will not solve UK’s growth dilemma
item.description: ‘We need a tram’ is a vote winner but skirts harder policy questions

item.title: Disruption expected as six-day doctors' strike begins
item.description: The NHS is advising patients in England to only use emergency services when necessary but attend any confirmed appointments.

item.title: Taiwan opposition leader visits China for expected meeting with Xi Jinping
item.description: Cheng Li-wun said she "gladly accepted" Xi Jinping's invitation and hopes to be a "bridge for peace".

item.title: Australia's most-decorated living soldier charged over alleged war crimes
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, who denies all wrongdoing, previously lost a landmark defamation case over the alleged murders.

item.title: Why Hungary’s Election Could Swing on Roma Votes
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s policies affecting the Roma minority have put those voters in play in upcoming parliamentary elections. In a tight race, they could make the difference.

item.title: India's top court hears challenges to ruling on women's entry into temple
item.description: Experts say the court's decision will have far-reaching consequences for women's religious freedoms in India.

item.title: Minnesota deny Rodriguez has rare muscle wasting condition
item.description: Minnesota United deny rumours that midfielder James Rodriguez is suffering from a potentially life-threatening muscle-wasting condition.

item.title: Taiwan opposition leader in ‘peace’ trip to China
item.description: First trip by a Kuomintang chair in a decade comes shortly ahead of planned Beijing summit with Donald Trump

item.title: Locals dispute Nigerian army claim of 31 rescued after Easter attack
item.description: Five civilians were killed by the gunmen and their bodies were recovered at the scene, the army said.

item.title: Vietnam gets its most powerful leader in decades
item.description: Communist Party leader To Lam’s election as president cements his grip on one of the world’s fastest-growing economies

item.title: Why Real Madrid tie could make or break Kane's Ballon d'Or hopes
item.description: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?

item.title: Don’t forget the Mag 7
item.description: Still a third of the S&P 500 — and wobbling a bit

item.title: SpaceX bankers bend over backwards to bag IPO role
item.description: To infinity back-scratching and beyond

item.title: JD Vance jets into Hungary to boost Orbán in final week of election race
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Brussels calls out Trump’s ‘illegal’ Iran threat

item.title: New Revelations Reignite Crypto Scandal Involving Argentina’s President Milei
item.description: Court documents raise questions about the Argentine president’s statements that he had no connection to the launch of the $Libra cryptocurrency.

item.title: Trump says US could destroy Iran ‘in one night’ as he demands opening of Hormuz
item.description: President steps up threats to target civil infrastructure if Tehran does not agree deal by Tuesday night

item.title: In Paris’s Catacombs, Can a Restoration Breathe New Life Into City’s Dead?
item.description: For centuries, the bones of some six million people were buried in the catacombs beneath the city. Curators are trying to preserve and modernize the tunnels while maintaining the spooky ambience.

item.title: Jamie Dimon angers private credit rivals . . . again
item.description: The JPMorgan chief warns that private credit losses will be larger than many expect

item.title: Top record labels and start-up Suno hit impasse in AI-generated music talks
item.description: One executive says there is ‘no path’ towards licensing deal under current proposal

item.title: Bezos’s Project Prometheus poaches xAI co-founder from OpenAI
item.description: Kyle Kosic joins Project Prometheus, the secretive start-up working on systems that can understand the physical world

item.title: Brussels banking rules could hit lenders and UK-EU ‘reset’, City figures warn
item.description: Concern that efforts to boost European defence spending could also be damaged

item.title: The future of global trade won’t depend on the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A new infrastructure is being built rapidly to avoid the strategic chokepoint and ensure global energy and food security

item.title: India's high-growth economy gets a Middle East oil shock
item.description: India's currency, stocks and growth projections take a beating as the country faces a triple energy shock due to Iran war.

item.title: Trump's deadline looms but Asian nations already have deals with Iran
item.description: Nations in the region have been keen to reach agreements as their economies are heavily reliant on Middle East energy.

item.title: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
item.description: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in Iran and the Middle East on Monday
item.description: President Trump escalated his threats ahead of his pending Tuesday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Pepsi Drops Sponsorship of Wireless Festival Headlined by Kanye West
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was “deeply concerned” that the rapper known for antisemitic and racist comments had been booked to perform at the Wireless Festival.

item.title: Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
item.description: The US president wants Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route, before his Tuesday deadline.

item.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
item.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

item.title: Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings
item.description: The father and son died in jail after being arrested on suspicion of breaching Covid lockdown rules in 2020.

item.title: Iran and America are spinning stories about the size of their victories
item.description: The bombast obscures the scale of the damage the war is doing

item.title: Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say
item.description: The Israeli strikes reportedly targeted Hamas security personnel after they clashed with an Israeli-backed militia near Maghazi camp.

item.title: Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
item.description: Several footballers have absconded in the past leaving behind what rights groups call a repressive government.

item.title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo's 'wise elder' dies aged 77
item.description: Albert Mazibuko sang in the iconic South African choral group for more than 55 years.

item.title: Epstein in Paris: How a Sex Offender Hustled for Access to France’s Elite
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein spent his last days of freedom in Paris, meeting with influential figures. It was a playbook he used everywhere he lived to stamp a veneer of respectability on a life of sordid criminality.

item.title: Churches and politicians in South Sudan call for 'lasting peace' in Easter messages
item.description: There are fears South Sudan may be plunged back into a civil war less than a decade after it ended.

item.title: Trump’s Board of Peace Gives Hamas Disarmament Deadline
item.description: The demand reflects both the U.S. administration’s eagerness to secure a lasting cease-fire in Gaza and its growing impatience with the Palestinian militant group.

item.title: 'We're sinking deeper': Iranians brace for infrastructure strikes as Trump deadline nears
item.description: Ordinary Iranians respond to the US president's threat to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: On Iran, Trump Keeps World Off Balance With Ever-Changing Threats
item.description: Global leaders are struggling in their efforts to find a way to end the American-Israeli war on Iran, and they are spooked about what President Trump might do next.

item.title: DR Congo agrees to take deportees from the US
item.description: The country will start receiving deportees from this month but has not said how many they will accept.

item.title: The strange, multicultural slang of Toronto’s teenagers
item.description: London rappers have an outsize influence

item.title: Taiwan and China are preparing for a summit, of sorts
item.description: A trip to the mainland by the KMT’s leader exposes a rift in the party

item.title: Somalia set for 'historic' first offshore oil drilling
item.description: A Turkish government-owned drilling ship is expected to arrive in Somali waters later this week.

item.title: How far do the European Union’s military ambitions go?
item.description: An interview with the EU’s top military officer

item.title: Pogacar may face fine for running red light in Flanders win
item.description: Tadej Pogacar wins a record-equalling third Tour of Flanders, but could face action after he is one of up to 20 riders who run a red light at a railway crossing.

item.title: Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh
item.description: More than 100 people, mostly children, have been killed by measles since mid-March, officials suspect.

item.title: A Hidden Russian Hand in Hungary’s Election? Actually, It’s Quite Open.
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orban has made hostility to Ukraine a centerpiece of his campaign. Moscow seems determined to repay the favor.

item.title: Why Trees Are Key to Russia’s Spring Offensive in Ukraine
item.description: In the age of drone warfare, Russia is expected to exploit the return of vegetation to help conceal its troops.

item.title: Trump issues expletive-laden threat to Iran over Hormuz Strait blockage
item.description: The US president says he will destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the vital waterway is not reopened.

item.title: Trump declares victory after rescue but threats to US operation in Iran still loom
item.description: The rescue could impact how Trump views a ground operation to take Kharg Island or to seize enriched uranium sites.

item.title: 'I adore her now': Mother learns to cope with child's autism in a country with little help
item.description: Malawian Martha Ongwane, brought low by her daughter's autism, found a rare support group.

item.title: Spain's huge pork industry seeks salvation from swine fever threat
item.description: Countries around the world, including the US, have already stopped imports over the outbreak.

item.title: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
item.description: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.

item.title: Hungary alleges plot to blow up gas pipeline ahead of election
item.description: It comes a week before votes are cast and follows warnings of so-called false flag operations blamed on Ukraine.

item.title: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
item.description: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

item.title: More Than 70 Migrants Missing After Boat Capsizes Off Libya
item.description: Survivors said at least 100 people were on a boat that had left Libya for Europe. Only 32 of those aboard are known to have survived.

item.title: The South American petro-state profiting from the Iran war
item.description: The boom is raising the risk that oil money devours the economy

item.title: Pope Leo calls for global leaders to choose peace in his first Easter Mass
item.description: Pope Leo XIV addressed thousands of worshippers gathered in St Peter's Square on Easter Sunday in his first address as pontiff.

item.title: European allies are losing hope of keeping America in NATO
item.description: Donald Trump is furious at their refusal to help his war against Iran

item.title: The tumbling rupee is a big problem for Narendra Modi
item.description: India’s prime minister knows the risks of a weak currency

item.title: China Built the World’s Drone Industry. Now It’s Locking Down the Skies.
item.description: The Chinese government tightened rules to curb what it described as illegal drone use, but some users say the changes are now restricting too many flights.

item.title: AI videos fuel rhetoric as Orbán bids for four more years in Hungary
item.description: Videos have targeted Viktor Orbán's election rival, who could unseat him after 16 years in office.

item.title: Comedians tell ministers lack of funding is no laughing matter
item.description: Culture Minister Ian Murray has agreed to work with the comedy industry to boost support after talks.

item.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
item.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

item.title: German males under 45 may need military approval for long stays abroad
item.description: Under the law, travel approvals must generally be granted and it remains unclear how the rule would be enforced if breached.

item.title: Russian attack on Ukraine market kills five
item.description: A Russian drone hit a busy spot in the southern Ukrainian town on Saturday morning, injuring another 21 people.
